title: Celebrating Amazing Autistic Minds
story: Every June 18, people around the world celebrate Autistic Pride Day to honor the unique strengths and talents of autistic individuals. This special day encourages everyone to learn about autism and appreciate how autistic people see and experience the world differently. Families, schools, and communities organize events where they share stories, create colorful art projects, and wear rainbow infinity symbols to show support. Many people post positive messages on social media using hashtags like #AutisticPrideDay to spread awareness and acceptance. The day reminds us that being different is something to celebrate, not hide, and that autistic people contribute amazing things to our world through their creativity, honesty, and special interests.
activity_question: What symbol do people often wear to show support on Autistic Pride Day?
follow_up_question: What unique talents or interests do you have that make you special?

title: Pack Your Basket for Picnic Day!
story: Every June 18, families around the world celebrate International Picnic Day by spreading blankets under trees and sharing meals outdoors! People pack sandwiches, fruit, and snacks in baskets or coolers, then head to parks, beaches, or even their own backyards. Some families play frisbee or card games between bites, while others just enjoy eating without walls around them. Schools sometimes organize special outdoor lunches, and friends post photos of their picnic spreads on social media. The best part? No dishes to wash afterward since most people use paper plates and cups for easy cleanup!
activity_question: What do people typically use to carry their food on International Picnic Day?
follow_up_question: What would be your dream picnic location and why?

title: Hook, Line, and Sinker Fun Day
story: Millions of people grab their fishing rods and head to lakes, rivers, and oceans on June 18 for National Go Fishing Day! Families pack snacks and spend the whole day by the water, trying to catch everything from tiny bluegill to massive bass. Some people fish from boats, others sit on docks or riverbanks with their feet dangling in the water. Even if you don't catch anything, it's still awesome because you get to be outside and maybe spot cool wildlife like herons or turtles. Many fishing stores offer free lessons and loaner gear to help beginners get started on this special day.
activity_question: What do many fishing stores offer on National Go Fishing Day to help beginners?
activity_choices: Magic bait that catches any fish
bonus_fact: You don't need a fishing license on this day in many states!

title: Splurge Day: Time to Treat Yourself!
story: Every June 18, people celebrate National Splurge Day by treating themselves to something special they normally wouldn't buy! Some folks splurge on fancy ice cream sundaes, expensive sneakers, or that video game they've been wanting forever. Others might buy flowers, get a massage, or order pizza with all their favorite toppings. The whole point is to break your usual budget rules for one day and do something nice for yourself. Social media fills up with photos of people's splurges, from tiny treats to big purchases, all shared with happy smiles!
activity_question: What do people typically do on National Splurge Day?
activity_choices: Save all their money in piggy banks
Treat themselves to something special they normally wouldn't buy
activity_answer: Treat themselves to something special they normally wouldn't buy
follow_up_question: What would be your perfect splurge if you celebrated this day?

title: Eating Green on the Scene
story: Chefs around the world put on their greenest aprons every June 18 for Sustainable Gastronomy Day! This special day celebrates cooking and eating in ways that protect our planet. Restaurants create menus using local ingredients that didn't travel far to reach your plate. Home cooks try recipes that use less water and energy. Food lovers share photos of colorful meals made from ingredients grown nearby, proving that eco-friendly food can be absolutely delicious and beautiful too!
activity_question: What do restaurants focus on during Sustainable Gastronomy Day?
activity_choices: Using local ingredients that didn't travel far
Serving only desserts all day long
Cooking with ingredients from other countries
activity_answer: Using local ingredients that didn't travel far
follow_up_question: What local ingredients could you use to make a planet-friendly meal?

title: Don't Panic About International Panic Day!
story: Every June 18, people around the world celebrate International Panic Day in the most relaxed way possible! This silly holiday encourages everyone to take a deep breath and laugh about all the little things that make us worry. Some people share funny panic stories on social media, while others practice calm-down techniques like counting to ten or doing yoga poses. Schools sometimes teach kids about managing stress through fun games and breathing exercises. The best part? You're supposed to NOT panic about anything today, making it the most wonderfully backwards holiday ever!
activity_question: What are people supposed to NOT do on International Panic Day?
follow_up_question: What's one thing that makes you worry that you could laugh about instead?
